授業方法: This course will be held face to face but may include some ONLINE content in both simultaneous two-way or on-demand formats.Students will engage in discussions with partners and small groups, make presentations, debate, and use English for communicative tasks.

トピック.授業計画1: Note: This syllabus may be subject to revisions according to student needs and abilities
トピック.授業外学習2:
Students must have regular access to computers and the internet either at home or through an on-campus computer lab. Online, on-demand or simultaneous interactive tasks may be required.

The amount of study time for homework will vary from week to week and will depend on 1) the content being reviewed, and 2) the ability of individual students to understand the content to complete tasks. However, students can expect to do a minimum of 1 hours and a maximum of 2 hours of study each week.

教科書/Required texts0:
DO NOT BUY second hand copies of the book. The digital ebook content will not be accessible / 中古本は購入しないでください。電子書籍のデジタルコンテンツにはアクセスできません。

Students MUST join the Google Classroom for this course (Google Classroom information will be given to students in the first lesson). Students can use a pre-existing Google account or make a new Google account. The Google account name must be changed to English if students use a preexisting account (First Name, Last Name, for example: Ken Watanabe)
教科書/Required texts1: Laurie Blass, Jessica Williams, Lynn Bonesteel, Christien Lee
21st Century Communication Student Book Split 2B with Spark + e-Book (1 year access)
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
Some course materials will be provided by the teacher

Also, students will need a Google account (Gmail) and regular access to the Internet to access Google Classroom

Dictionaries (electronic, smartphone, or paper) highly recommended
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。
各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。

成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Classroom Activities 30%, Presentations 30%, Overall Engagement 10%, Individual Assignments - Homework/Quizzes/Journal 30%
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note Grade assessment may be subject to revisions according to student needs and abilities at the teacher's discretion――――――――出席、遅刻の制度については以下URL先をご確認ください。/ Please refer to the following URL for details regarding the attendance and tardiness policy.https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/area/detail?areaId=7144&directLink=1――――――――
備考Note0: If students are late to class, they are considered tardy. If more than 30 minutes late, they are considered absent. Two instances of tardiness are counted as one absence. One point per tardiness and two points per absence is taken off the final grade. A combination of 4 absences / 8 tardiness will result in a failing grade and the students expulsion from the class.
